skill.name: safe-debug
skill.description: Conservative debugging skill for deep learning research. Diagnoses tracebacks, CUDA OOM, checkpoint mismatches, and training failures without auto-patching code.
skill.summary: Conservative deep learning research debugging with diagnosis-first workflow and explicit approval before any code changes.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: The skill is a conservative debugging aid for deep learning research. Static analysis flagged 22 potential issues, but all are false positives: the 'Ruby/shell backtick' detections matched Python f-strings and markdown formatting, the 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags found no actual cryptography in the codebase, and the 'path traversal' flags matched relative path references in markdown documentation. The Python script uses only standard library modules (argparse, json, pathlib) with no subprocess, eval, or network access. No security concerns were found.

content.value_statement: Deep learning debugging often leads to speculative patches that break research reproducibility. This skill provides conservative diagnosis with explicit approval gates before any code modification, keeping debug fixes separate from research contributions.
actual_capabilities: Classifies errors into categories (CUDA OOM, checkpoint mismatch, shape mismatch, NaN loss, device mismatch, distributed issues, file missing, runtime failures).
Generates DIAGNOSIS.md with error excerpts and conservative analysis.
Produces PATCH_PLAN.md with smallest safe fix suggestions and savepoint recommendations.
Outputs status.json tracking patch authorization state (always unauthorized by default).
Operates as a read-only diagnostic tool that never modifies repository code without explicit approval.
limitations: Cannot automatically apply patches; all fixes require explicit human approval.
Classification is keyword-based and may not catch novel or complex error patterns.
Does not execute or run training code; it only analyzes error text.
Focused on deep learning research workflows; less applicable to general software debugging.

use_cases.title: Diagnose CUDA OOM Errors
use_cases.description: A researcher hits a CUDA out-of-memory error during training. The skill categorizes it and suggests checking batch size and mixed-precision settings before any code changes.

use_cases.title: Audit Checkpoint Loading Failures
use_cases.description: A PhD student loads a checkpoint and gets size mismatch errors. The skill diagnoses the root cause and recommends verifying checkpoint source before introducing compatibility code.
use_cases.target_user: Graduate students working with pre-trained models

use_cases.title: Safe NaN Loss Investigation
use_cases.description: Training loss goes to NaN mid-run. The skill flags it as a savepoint-required change and suggests inspecting data ranges and learning rate before modifying architecture.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Error Diagnosis
prompt_templates.prompt: Use safe-debug to diagnose this error: RuntimeError: CUDA out of memory. Tried to allocate 2.00 GiB.

prompt_templates.title: Checkpoint Mismatch Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Diagnose with safe-debug: RuntimeError: Error(s) in loading state_dict for ResNet50: Missing key(s) in state_dict: fc.weight, fc.bias.
prompt_templates.scenario: Loading a pre-trained model with architecture mismatch

prompt_templates.title: NaN Loss Investigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply safe-debug to this symptom: Training loss is NaN after 3 epochs. Loss is nan, not converging. The model is a transformer with mixed precision enabled.
prompt_templates.scenario: Investigating training instability with mixed precision

prompt_templates.title: Full Traceback Diagnosis
prompt_templates.prompt: Run safe-debug on this full traceback with --error-file error.txt. I need a diagnosis, likely cause category, and conservative fix suggestions before any code changes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive error analysis with patch plan

output_examples.input: RuntimeError: CUDA out of memory. Tried to allocate 1.50 GiB.
output: - Check effective batch size, input resolution, and mixed-precision settings before patching model code.
- Prefer a configuration-only reduction before touching architecture.

output_examples.input: RuntimeError: size mismatch for fc.weight: copying a param with shape torch.Size([1000, 2048]) from checkpoint, the shape in current model is torch.Size([10, 2048]).
output: - Verify checkpoint source, model variant, and load strictness assumptions.
- Confirm whether the mismatch is expected before introducing compatibility code.

best_practices: Always run safe-debug before making any code changes to get a structured diagnosis first.
Create a savepoint or branch before applying any medium-risk or high-risk patches recommended by the skill.
Keep debug fixes separate from research contributions to maintain experiment reproducibility.
Provide full tracebacks via --error-file for more accurate classification.
anti_patterns: Do not use safe-debug for broad refactoring or speculative code adaptation tasks.
Do not apply patches without first reviewing the PATCH_PLAN.md output and getting explicit approval.
Do not use this skill for general repository walkthroughs without an active failure symptom.

faq.question: What types of errors can safe-debug diagnose?
faq.answer: The skill classifies CUDA OOM, checkpoint mismatches, distributed training issues, device mismatches, shape mismatches, NaN losses, file missing errors, and general runtime failures.

faq.question: Does safe-debug automatically fix code?
faq.answer: No. The skill only diagnoses and suggests fixes. All patches require explicit human approval before any code modification.

faq.question: How do I use safe-debug with my error?
faq.answer: Provide the error text via --error-text flag or save it to a file and use --error-file. The skill outputs DIAGNOSIS.md, PATCH_PLAN.md, and status.json.

faq.question: Is safe-debug suitable for production systems?
faq.answer: It is designed for deep learning research workflows. It focuses on conservative diagnosis rather than automated production error handling.

faq.question: Does the skill execute any external commands?
faq.answer: No. safe-debug is a read-only analysis tool that uses only Python standard library modules to classify and document errors.

faq.question: Can I use safe-debug with Codex or Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as listed in its supported tools.
